summary: Germany will add €300 million for 50,000 long‑range munitions to Ukraine. Russian drones are striking logistics targets in the Dnipropetrovsk region. Bulgaria announced it will cease weapons deliveries to Ukraine.
key_points: Germany commits an extra €300 million to supply 50,000 long‑range munitions to Ukraine (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30794).
Zelensky asked the UK to redirect £2.4 billion from Abramovich’s Chelsea sale to buy air‑defence systems (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30786).
Russian drones are targeting trucks and gas stations on the Pavlograd‑Pokrovsk highways in Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30784).
Bulgaria’s defence minister announced the country will no longer supply weapons to Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/81799).
Russian air‑defence forces reported shooting down 292 Ukrainian UAVs in one day (Source: @tass_agency/379946).
Ukrainian Ministry of Defence plans an amnesty for AWOL soldiers with contract options of 10, 14 or 24 months (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30773).
Ukrainian disabled arm‑wrestlers were fined €250 each for staying on stage during the Russian anthem (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30778).
Kiev‑region air‑raid alarm sounded at 20:27 on 09 June 2026 (Source: @eRadarrua/96055).

sections.executive_summary: Germany’s €300 million pledge for 50,000 long‑range munitions marks the most significant recent aid boost. Russian UAV activity continues, striking logistics corridors in Dnipropetrovsk. Bulgaria’s decision to halt weapons shipments reduces Ukraine’s supply lines.

points: The Ukrainian Ministry of Defence announced an amnesty for AWOL personnel, allowing them to sign new contracts of 10, 14 or 24 months with criminal liability removed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30773).
Zelensky met with British officials, urging the confiscation of £2.4 billion from Roman Abramovich’s Chelsea sale to fund air‑defence purchases (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30786).
Ukraine will host a military parade in France on 14 July featuring two Mirage 2000 jets flown by Ukrainian and French pilots, one painted in Ukrainian colours (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30800).
Ukrainian disabled arm‑wrestlers were fined €250 each for leaving the podium during the Russian anthem at a European championship in Budapest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30778).
Russian air‑defence reported downing 292 Ukrainian UAVs in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/379946).
Russian drones are targeting logistics in Dnipropetrovsk, striking trucks and gas stations on the Pavlograd‑Pokrovsk highways (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30784).
Three explosions occurred on a main gas pipeline in Dagestan, with a pipe rupture cited as a preliminary cause of a fire (Source: @tass_agency/379954).
Russian forces have equipped Kalibr missiles with a cassette warhead, re‑using foreign components for guidance heads (Source: @UkraineNow/81796).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over the Ocheretyn district of Sumy heading west (Source: @kpszsu/64644).
Strike‑capable UAVs were observed over northern Chernihiv heading west (Source: @kpszsu/64620).
Groups of hostile UAVs were seen over eastern Zaporizhzhia heading toward the city (Source: @kpszsu/64622).
UAVs from northern Kharkiv were observed heading toward Kharkiv city (Source: @kpszsu/64657).
A hostile reconnaissance UAV was spotted in northern Chernihiv (Source: @kpszsu/64646).
UAV activity reported in Poltava district moving north‑west (Source: @kpszsu/64656).
Guided aviation bomb launches were recorded over Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/64640).
Guided aviation bomb launches were also recorded over Kharkiv (Source: @kpszsu/64639).
UAVs from northern Kherson moved toward the south‑east of Mykolaiv (Source: @kpszsu/64626).
UAV groups in south‑east Dnipropetrovsk headed toward Pavlograd (Source: @kpszsu/64622).
UAVs from the south‑east were observed moving toward the Dnipro River (Source: @kpszsu/64650).
UAV activity reported in the south‑west of Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @eRadarrua/96037).
Russian air‑defence claimed 292 Ukrainian UAVs shot down in one day (Source: @tass_agency/379946).
Ukrainian air‑raid alarm sounded in Kyiv at 20:27 on 09 June 2026 (Source: @eRadarrua/96055).
Russian drones targeting logistics in Dnipropetrovsk include attacks on trucks and gas stations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30784).
Guided aviation bomb launches reported over Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv (Source: @kpszsu/64640, @kpszsu/64639).

key_developments: Germany will allocate an additional €300 million to deliver 50,000 long‑range munitions to Ukraine (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30794).
Zelensky asked the UK to confiscate £2.4 billion from Abramovich’s Chelsea sale for Ukrainian air‑defence purchases (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30786).
Russian drones are striking trucks and gas stations on the Pavlograd‑Pokrovsk highways in Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30784).
Bulgaria’s defence minister announced the country will cease weapons deliveries to Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/81799).
Russian air‑defence reported downing 292 Ukrainian UAVs in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/379946).
Three explosions hit a major gas pipeline in Dagestan, with pipe rupture identified as a preliminary fire cause (Source: @tass_agency/379954).
Ukrainian Ministry of Defence plans an amnesty for AWOL soldiers with new contract options (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30773).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian UAV activity remains intense across multiple fronts, targeting logistics and civilian infrastructure, while Russia’s air‑defence claims high UAV kill rates. The introduction of cassette warheads on Kalibr missiles suggests a shift toward area‑effect strikes. Ukraine’s new long‑range munition supply from Germany could enhance its deep‑strike capability, but the loss of Bulgarian arms shipments may strain ammunition stocks.
sections.regional_response: Western partners continue to discuss financial mechanisms, exemplified by Zelensky’s request to redirect Abramovich’s funds for air‑defence. Germany’s funding boost signals sustained EU support, while Bulgaria’s policy reversal reflects divergent EU member stances. NATO allies are monitoring increased Russian UAV pressure on eastern logistics corridors.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitor the deployment of German‑supplied long‑range munitions for changes in strike patterns. Track Russian UAV flight paths reported by local sources for potential targeting of supply routes. Assess the impact of Bulgaria’s aid halt on Ukrainian ammunition reserves and seek alternative supply channels.
